one_line_ask: Prep the Lanternwood Folk Fest sponsor logos (vectorize / transparent-cut) and grade the performer press photos, source a kraft cover texture, then run the client's authored multi-page InDesign booklet template through two data-merge passes against the schedule and sponsor CSVs to produce a press-ready PDF and a screen-quality PDF program guide.
brief: Lanternwood Folk Fest, a fictional two-day roots-and-Americana music festival, needs its official program booklet finished for print and for the event app. The organizer has handed off an authored multi-page InDesign booklet template (folkfest_program_template.indd) that already carries the festival's established warm-cream / lantern-amber colour scheme and type hierarchy, with real InDesign data-merge fields laid out across the interior spreads (<<day>>, <<stage>>, <<set_time>>, <<artist>>, <<bio>>, <<artist_photo>> for the schedule pages, and <<sponsor_name>>, <<sponsor_tier>>, <<sponsor_logo>> for the sponsor grid on the back-cover spread), plus front/back covers with a full-bleed frame for a kraft-paper texture and a placeholder for the festival wordmark. Along with the template the organizer supplies: a schedule CSV (one row per performance set: day, stage, set time, artist, one-line bio, and the filename of that artist's press photo), a sponsor CSV (sponsor name, tier — Presenting / Stage / Community — and the filename of that sponsor's logo), a folder of raw sponsor logo files (some are clean flat rasters, some are photos of the logo on a busy background, some are already re
